prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "A-Skip 跑姿练习" (A-skip running drill) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top phase of an A-skip drill: right foot on the ball of the foot pushing off the ground; left thigh driven up to parallel with the floor (knee at hip height), left knee bent at 90 degrees with shin perpendicular and toes pulled up (dorsiflexed); arms in opposition like running; torso upright with slight forward lean; this is the classic A-skip drill posture used by sprinters; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hip flexors of the lifted leg and calf muscles of the pushing leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and tibialis anterior (front of shin). Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors of the lifted leg; "小腿三头肌" pointing to the calf muscles of the pushing leg; "胫骨前肌 / 臀大肌" pointing to the tibialis anterior and glute area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
